organization: Global Water and Sanitation Centre (GWSC), AIT
description: Managing geospatial datasets and creating GIS visualizations for water and sanitation projects
responsibilities: Prepare and manage geospatial datasets for sanitation infrastructure, service coverage, and urban demographics.
Generate ArcGIS/QGIS maps for CWIS city assessments, investment planning, and presentations.
Integrate field data and geo-referenced surveys into project dashboards and planning tools.
Assist in developing technical reports communicating GIS outputs to international organizations.

description: Graduate research focusing on hydrological modelling and water security
responsibilities: Developing SWAT hydrological models for water resource assessment
Research on climate change impacts on water availability

description: Developed hydrological models for flood risk assessment in Northern Thailand
responsibilities: Developed SWAT hydrological models for water resource assessment and flood risk in Northern Thailand.
Designed a water security framework and identified nature-based solutions for urban water management.
Organized research partner meetings and served as liaison across teams.

description: Led suspended sediment monitoring project with citizen science team
responsibilities: Established a suspended sediment laboratory from scratch; deployed OBS sensors at remote Himalayan sites.
Led all fieldwork planning, data collection, and quality control operations.
Recruited, trained, and managed a citizen science team to expand monitoring network capacity.
Co-authored findings at AGU Fall Meeting 2023 and WaterSciCon24, published in EGUsphere (2026).
Facilitated workshops for local partners, building long-term monitoring capacity.
